name: Sepp
description: Production-proven patterns for .NET and Akka.NET development. Each skill is standalone and focuses on one concern.

skills.name: complexity-guardian
skills.description: Review code for over-engineering and under-engineering. Detect unnecessary abstractions, god classes, missing error handling, and premature patterns. Use during code review, after changes, or when asking 'is this too complex?'. TRIGGER when: user asks about complexity, requests code review, says 'simplify', 'too complex', 'refactor', or 'clean up'.

skills.name: actor-pattern-library
skills.description: Generate Akka.NET actor implementations following production-proven patterns: Persistent Actor, Cluster Singleton, Sharded Entity, Stashing Actor, Gateway/Proxy, Request-Response with Timeout, Leader-Only Router. Includes anti-patterns to avoid. TRIGGER when: creating or refactoring Akka.NET actors, asking about actor patterns, or implementing stateful concurrent entities.

skills.name: message-driven-designer
skills.description: Design messages, events, and commands for actor systems and distributed architectures. Covers Command/Event/Query separation, Envelope pattern, immutability, pub/sub vs direct messaging, EventHub with type hierarchy, versioning, and correlation. TRIGGER when: designing messages, events, commands, or communication patterns between actors or services.

skills.name: state-machine-designer
skills.description: Design explicit state machines with event-sourced state, transition guards, and side-effect isolation. Covers state modeling, transitions, recovery, Akka.NET Become() integration, and visualization in Mermaid/DOT. TRIGGER when: dealing with lifecycle management, complex state transitions, workflow orchestration, or entity state tracking.

skills.name: resilience-patterns
skills.description: Implement fault tolerance patterns: Supervision strategies, Mitigation/Fallback actors, Stashing for availability, Circuit Breaker, Retry with Backoff, Startup Dependency Retry. For Akka.NET actors and distributed .NET services. TRIGGER when: implementing error handling, retry logic, fault tolerance, graceful degradation, or supervision hierarchies.

skills.name: code-complexity-analyzer
skills.description: Analyze code complexity metrics: Cyclomatic Complexity, CRAP Score, coupling, abstraction depth, method length. Provides concrete recommendations for splitting, inlining, or simplifying. TRIGGER when: asking 'is this too complex?', during refactoring, reviewing metrics, or assessing code health.

skills.name: app-startup-orchestrator
skills.description: Design application startup orchestration with interface-based composition, health check registries, startup tasks, retry loops, and tag-based Kubernetes probes. For ASP.NET Core and Akka.NET services. TRIGGER when: setting up app bootstrapping, configuring health checks, implementing startup tasks, or designing k8s probe endpoints.

skills.name: domain-modeling-patterns
skills.description: Design domain models with value objects, record hierarchies, complex type parsers, and attribute-driven discovery. Make illegal states unrepresentable. TRIGGER when: creating domain entities, modeling value objects, designing type hierarchies, or asking 'how should I model X'.

skills.name: infrastructure-patterns
skills.description: Production infrastructure patterns: MongoDB builder/factory, HTTP client resilience with Polly, configuration with environment overrides, pagination, caching. TRIGGER when: setting up database access, configuring HTTP clients, implementing caching, pagination, or configuration management.

skills.name: observability-patterns
skills.description: Implement observability for .NET services: Structured logging with Serilog, correlation IDs, health check endpoints, OpenTelemetry tracing, Prometheus metrics. TRIGGER when: setting up logging, tracing, metrics, health endpoints, or asking about monitoring and observability.
